rule.name: Insecure HTTP Requests
rule.rule_desc: Detects network request usage that may involve insecure HTTP transport.
rule.vuln_desc: Cleartext network traffic can be intercepted or modified by attackers.
rule.developer: Enforce HTTPS endpoints and reject insecure transport for production traffic.
rule.reviewer: Inspect request construction and endpoint policy for transport security controls. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Weak Local Secret Storage
rule.rule_desc: Detects persistence APIs commonly used for local settings storage.
rule.vuln_desc: Storing secrets in plain settings storage can expose credentials/tokens on compromised devices.
rule.developer: Store secrets in platform secure storage plugins.
rule.reviewer: Verify secret material is not persisted in application settings APIs. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: WebView Usage with Dynamic Sources
rule.rule_desc: Detects embedded WebView usage that requires trusted source enforcement.
rule.vuln_desc: Loading untrusted content in WebViews can enable malicious script execution or phishing flows.
rule.developer: Restrict sources to trusted origins and sanitize dynamic navigation paths.
rule.reviewer: Review WebView content source controls and navigation policies. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: NativeScript Debug/Dev Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects development mode markers in NativeScript projects.
rule.vuln_desc: Debug settings may expose diagnostics and weaken runtime posture.
rule.developer: Ensure release builds disable debug mode.
rule.reviewer: Review build/runtime environment toggles. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: NativeScript Insecure HTTP Usage
rule.rule_desc: Detects cleartext endpoint usage in NativeScript code/config.
rule.vuln_desc: Cleartext traffic can expose sensitive network data.
rule.developer: Use HTTPS endpoints and secure transport defaults.
rule.reviewer: Audit endpoint declarations and request helpers. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: NativeScript WebView Script Execution Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects WebView and dynamic script execution patterns.
rule.vuln_desc: Untrusted WebView/script execution can increase injection risk.
rule.developer: Restrict content origins and harden JS bridge interactions.
rule.reviewer: Review webview bindings and message handlers. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: NativeScript Local Storage Secret Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects potentially sensitive values written to local storage APIs.
rule.vuln_desc: Improper local persistence can leak credentials/tokens.
rule.developer: Use secure storage primitives for secrets.
rule.reviewer: Check storage keys and data classification. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: NativeScript TLS Bypass Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects certificate/trust bypass indicators in networking code.
rule.vuln_desc: TLS bypass weakens channel security and enables MITM attacks.
rule.reviewer: Audit HTTP client trust configuration. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: NativeScript Hardcoded Secret Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects likely hardcoded secrets in NativeScript projects.
rule.vuln_desc: Hardcoded credentials can be extracted from app artifacts.
rule.developer: Avoid embedding secrets and rotate compromised values.
rule.reviewer: Confirm no production secrets exist in source/config. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Android Debuggable Build Enabled
rule.rule_desc: Detects debug-enabled Android build settings in NativeScript projects.
rule.vuln_desc: Debuggable builds can expose runtime internals and facilitate reverse engineering.
rule.developer: Disable debugging for release builds and verify production build variants.
rule.reviewer: Confirm release pipeline produces non-debuggable APK/AAB artifacts. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: iOS ATS Relaxed Settings
rule.rule_desc: Detects ATS policy relaxation in iOS settings.
rule.vuln_desc: Relaxed ATS settings can expose app communication to interception.
rule.developer: Keep ATS defaults and scope exceptions tightly to trusted endpoints.
rule.reviewer: Validate ATS exceptions in iOS configuration files. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.
